Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Dusko Licanin
Message:
feat(dispatch): phase 14.2 PR β — thread cols + RFC 8058 + daily-cap env (#322)

* feat(dispatch): Phase 14.2 PR β — campaign_messages thread cols + RFC 8058 + daily-cap env

Three concerns shipped together because they're all "what the message
row needs to know about itself before the webhook handler arrives":

1. Thread / idempotency columns on `campaign_messages`
   - thread_id TEXT — provider-side conversation key (webhook attributes
     replies via this)
   - in_reply_to_message_id TEXT — previously-sent message in the same
     thread; NULL on initial touch
   - tracking_id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() UNIQUE — LDS-side opaque
     identifier for the unsubscribe-link binding
   Each backed by a partial index on its only lookup shape (NOT NULL
   predicate to skip the pending/initial-touch pile).

2. RFC 8058 List-Unsubscribe-Post endpoint
   - `GET /unsubscribe/{token}` renders a minimal HTML confirmation
     form; POSTs back to the same URL.
   - `POST /unsubscribe/{token}` verifies HMAC + timestamp window,
     dereferences tracking_id → campaign_messages → leads.email,
     inserts a suppression row with channel='all' and
     reason='unsubscribe', returns 200; any verification failure
     returns 410 Gone with a generic body (never leaks which stage
     failed).
   - Token format: 2-byte version + 32-byte payload (tracking_id u128 BE
     + issued_at u32 BE + 12 reserved) + 32-byte HMAC-SHA256. URL-safe
     base64, no padding. 90-day TTL. ±5-min clock skew tolerance.
     `hmac.compare_digest` everywhere.
   - Public (no X-API-Key); slowapi 10/min/IP. The recipient is not an
     LDS user.
   - Lives in `backend/main.py` alongside the other public routes
     (`/`, `/metrics`) — `src/api/routes/` doesn't exist in this
     monolithic-main.py codebase. Spec's path was advisory.

3. Daily cap + send-window env config
   - `src/utils/dispatch_policy.py` exposes a frozen DispatchPolicy
     snapshot loaded ONCE at module import from os.environ. Multi-
     worker uvicorn races mid-request env mutation; this is the only
     legitimate read-path. Test code reloads via `_reload_for_testing()`.
   - 6 env vars: EMAIL_DAILY_CAP_PER_MAILBOX (30), EMAIL_WARMUP_PER_MAILBOX
     (10), SEND_WINDOW_START (09:00), SEND_WINDOW_END (17:00), SEND_DAYS
     (mon-fri), SEND_TIMEZONE_MODE (lead).
   - Every parse fails-safe — malformed values fall back to the default
     instead of bricking boot.
   - `is_send_day()` + `is_in_window()` helpers. Lexicographic compare
     on zero-padded HH:MM strings.

Plus:
- `InstantlyLeadPayload.from_lds_lead()` now accepts `list_unsubscribe`
  and threads it as two custom variables (`list_unsubscribe` +
  `list_unsubscribe_post`) so Instantly's custom-vars-to-header bridge
  writes the RFC 8058 headers on send. Pinned `LDS_KEYS` test updated.
- Schema-drift gate: split the campaign_messages ALTER into three
  separate single-column statements so the drift regex catches each
  added column (multi-clause ALTER only matches the first).

Naming kept from spec: thread_id, in_reply_to_message_id, tracking_id
(matches the JSON columns RFC 8058 + Instantly use, plus the wider
"thread" taxonomy across the dispatch industry). `provider_message_id`
(from PR #319) stays — spec's `vendor_message_id` was a fresh-eyes
rename without enough justification to churn the drift gate.

Tests: 64/64 pass locally (33 new + 31 PR-α regression).
- 16 unit tests on the token util (round-trip, tampering, expiry, clock
  skew, secret-resolution corner cases)
- 12 unit tests on dispatch policy (env fallback, window/day-of-week
  boundary)
- 9 endpoint integration tests (TestClient, mocked DB, 410 generic-body
  assertion, suppression row write-shape pinned)

Depends-on: #321 (PR α — suppressions table must exist before this PR's
unsubscribe handler can INSERT into it).

* ci: force re-trigger

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Duško Ličanin
Message:
docs: TEST_RESULTS audit + remediation log

Records the /vibe-security audit + live verification rounds:
- Static review (auth, SSRF, CSP, prompt injection)
- Live DB audit found 3 CRITICAL on Supabase (permissive RLS, missing
  RLS on orchestration_jobs, anon-callable SECURITY DEFINER RPC)
  → all closed via migrations (lockdown_public_tables_and_rpcs,
  create_campaigns_and_messages)
- Live header re-test post-fix (CSP, HSTS, no Server, no X-Powered-By,
  Cache-Control no-store on proxy)
- Authed E2E (sign-out, /leads/clear admin gate, prompt-injection
  empirical defense)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Uncovered a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive user data.
Author: google-labs-jules[bot]
Message:
Add testing for APIError in list_leads endpoint

Co-authored-by: 3443434525234 <128933179+3443434525234@users.noreply.github.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Duško Ličanin
Message:
Initial commit: LeadDataScraper full project with structured logging

- Backend API (FastAPI) with lead management, SEO audit, enrichment, discovery
- Frontend (Next.js) dashboard
- Structured logging via Python logging module (replaces all print() calls)
- Standardized error responses with proper HTTP status codes
- Error handling for all Gemini AI calls in agentic_router
- Rotating file logs (logs/leadscraper.log, 5MB, 3 backups)
- Supabase integration with auto-migration support
- Campaign management, outreach draft generation
- Parallel auditor with timeout handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
